text.title: App Store Optimization (ASO) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding App Store Optimization (ASO). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One spec per app per store. Cross-store specs blur the constraints.
rule.rationale: Apple + Play fields differ; mixing breaks both.
Below 5 traffic narrow; above 10 dilution dominates.

rule.statement: Title carries the highest-volume keyword in the first 30 chars.
First 3 screenshots carry the value prop; >85% of users never swipe further.
Review prompt fires AFTER a success event (level cleared, task completed) — never on cold open.
rule.rationale: Cold-open prompts produce ratings tank + Apple denial.

rule.statement: Re-baseline keywords + assets every 30 days. Store algorithms drift; static specs decay.
rule.rationale: Static specs lose rank over months.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-app-store-optimization.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
